auth.haveAccount: Already have an account? Log in

profiles.title: Profiles
profiles.name: Name
profiles.none: No profiles yet — create one to start.
profiles.deleteConfirm: Delete this profile? Its data sources must be removed first.

positions.title: Positions
positions.noPositions: No positions match the current filters.
positions.confirmDelete: Delete this position? It will disappear everywhere and won't return on sync.

dataSources.title: Data sources
dataSources.label: Label
dataSources.readOnlyHint: Use a READ-ONLY API key (no trading, no withdrawal). Credentials are encrypted at rest and never shown again.
dataSources.noCredentials: No API keys — add them to sync
dataSources.disabledToast: Source disabled — existing data kept, no new data
dataSources.syncFromHint: Earliest date to import. Leave blank to pull as far back as the exchange allows. This can't be changed later.
dataSources.deleteConfirm: Delete this data source and its positions?

coverage.BITUNIX: Full history since you opened the account.
coverage.BINGX: Only ~the last 30 days — BingX's API serves no older fills, so sync regularly; older trades can't be recovered.
coverage.BITMART: Recent history only — BitMart's API serves a limited window of fills, so sync regularly; older trades can't be recovered. Only the API key is needed (read-only).
coverage.QUANTFURY: Your entire history — whatever is in the uploaded PDF (no API, so nothing syncs automatically).
coverage.JOURNAL_CSV: Whatever you import — a manual CSV of closed positions, e.g. from exchanges that no longer exist. Nothing syncs automatically.

journalCsv.hint: Upload a CSV of closed positions in tradelog's canonical format (below). Ideal for hand-kept journals or dead exchanges. Re-uploading the same file is safe (duplicates are updated in place).
journalCsv.warnClosedBeforeOpened: {{count}} row(s) have a close date earlier than the open date — check those entries.
journalCsv.warnOverlap: {{count}} row(s) look like positions already imported from another source in this profile. Importing them would double-count their PnL.

format.ai_hint: Tip: paste this whole reference into any AI assistant (ChatGPT, Claude, Gemini, …) together with your old trade history — a screenshot, a PDF statement, or a messy spreadsheet from an exchange that no longer exists — and ask it to produce a CSV that follows exactly this format. Then come back here, Preview to validate, and Import.
format.intro: Dialect: UTF-8, semicolon (;) column separator, comma (,) or dot (.) decimal mark, RFC-4180 quoting (wrap a field in double quotes if it contains ;, " or a line break). First row is the literal header below; one closed position per row. Lines starting with # are ignored.
format.pnlNote: realized_pnl is the result BEFORE fees (gross). Leave it empty to have it computed as (exit_price − entry_price) × quantity, negated for shorts. Put commissions in fees.
format.notionalNote: No per-unit price? Set quantity to 1 and use the TOTAL position value as entry_price (amount invested / notional) and exit_price (amount returned) — not your margin.

cols.symbol: Base asset or BASE/QUOTE, e.g. BTC or SUI/USDT. Quote defaults to USDT.
cols.opened_at: Open date: YYYY-MM-DD, or an instant YYYY-MM-DDTHH:MM:SSZ (UTC).
cols.closed_at: Close date, same format as opened_at.
cols.quantity: Base-asset units. Use 1 if you only have totals. Defaults to 1.
cols.entry_price: Per-unit entry price — or total invested when quantity=1.
cols.exit_price: Per-unit exit price — or total returned when quantity=1.
cols.realized_pnl: Gross result before fees. Empty = computed from prices.
cols.exchange: Venue this trade came from (e.g. FTX). Becomes the position's exchange; if blank, this data source's label is used.

quantfury.title: Quantfury PDF import
quantfury.hint: Upload the Quantfury "Trading History Report" PDF. Closed positions are parsed and imported; re-uploading is safe (duplicates are skipped).

taxonomy.title: Tags (Origen)
taxonomy.hint: Customize the tags you can assign to positions. Each group is single-select per position.

settings.timeZoneHint: Used to group analytics by hour of day and weekday in your local time.

capital.title: Trading capital & risk
capital.exchangesHint: Your current balance on each exchange. Entered manually for now; there's no history — saving overwrites the previous value.
capital.noExchanges: No exchanges yet. Add a data source or import a trade history first.
capital.riskHint: The two percentages of capital you're willing to risk on a single trade. Used to show the maximum to lose per trade on the dashboard.

backup.title: Backup & restore
backup.description: Download a portable JSON copy of everything in your account — profiles, tags, and your full position history with fills and annotations. API keys are not included.
backup.restoreHint: Restoring replaces ALL of your current data with the file's contents. Use it to rebuild from scratch; you'll re-enter API keys afterwards.
backup.replaceWarning: This replaces ALL your current profiles, data sources and positions with the contents of this file. API keys are not in the backup — you'll re-enter them afterwards to resume syncing. This cannot be undone.
backup.imported: Restored {{profiles}} profile(s) and {{positions}} position(s). Re-enter your API keys to resume syncing.
backup.invalidFile: That file isn't a valid tradelog backup.

analytics.title: Dashboard
analytics.noData: No closed positions yet. Add a data source or import a trade history.

capital.title: Trading capital & risk
capital.info: Your current balance and the maximum to lose per trade. This is a current balance — it uses the Exchange filter only and does not change with the Period or Origen filters.
capital.empty: No trading capital set. Add it in Settings → Trading capital & risk.
capital.capitalInfo: Sum of your current capital across the selected exchange(s).
capital.maxLossInfo: Capital × risk %. The maximum to lose on a single trade — not the position size.

stats.title: Statistics
stats.info: Key figures over the filtered set of closed trades. PnL is net of fees and funding.
stats.totalPnlInfo: Sum of the net result of every trade in the set.
stats.volumeInfo: Total notional traded across the set, counting both legs: quantity × (entry price + exit price), summed over every trade.
stats.winRateInfo: Share of trades with a positive result. Break-even trades (0) are excluded.
stats.avgWinInfo: Mean net result of winning trades.
stats.avgLossInfo: Mean net result of losing trades.
stats.expectancyInfo: Expected result per trade: (win rate × average win) − (loss rate × |average loss|).
stats.avgRRInfo: Average win divided by the absolute average loss.

analytics.cumulativeProfitInfo: Running sum of net results ordered by close date — your equity curve.
analytics.activityInfo: Count of long and short trades per day, with each day's win rate.
analytics.pnlPerDayInfo: Total net result for each day of the month.
analytics.monthlySummaryInfo: Net result per month with the monthly win rate.
analytics.winRateByHourInfo: Win rate and number of trades for each hour of the day, in your time zone.
analytics.winRateByWeekdayInfo: Win rate and number of trades for each weekday, in your time zone.
analytics.directionInfo: Long vs short: number of trades, win rate, total PnL and expectancy.
analytics.traderStyleInfo: Trades grouped by holding time: scalper (< 15m), day trader (15m–24h), swing (> 24h).
analytics.winningStreaksInfo: Average winning-streak length and the net PnL of the longest winning streak.
analytics.losingStreaksInfo: Average losing-streak length and the net PnL of the longest losing streak.
analytics.recoveryInfo: How often the trade after a loss is a win.
analytics.calendarInfo: Total net PnL for each day of the month, colored by sign.
analytics.mostTradedInfo: Pairs ranked by number of trades.
analytics.mostProfitableInfo: Pairs ranked by positive total PnL.
analytics.leastProfitableInfo: Pairs ranked by negative total PnL.
analytics.feesInfo: Total fees for each day of the month.
analytics.cumulativeFeesInfo: Running sum of fees over time.
analytics.feeRatioInfo: Per-day return on fees: net PnL ÷ fees. Month value is month PnL ÷ month fees, from monthly totals. Days with no trades or zero fees are skipped.

sync.skipped: Sync already in progress — try again shortly

quickSync.label: Quick sync
quickSync.noExchanges: No exchange sources to sync. Add Bitunix or BingX in Settings.
